In a remote bay in southwest Alaska, a native legend tells of a beast that shakes the ocean and destroys those who walk its shores.

In 1958, a local fisherman came face to face with that beast, a mysterious wave more than twice the height of the Golden Gate Bridge. Plavka and his colleague noticed that a band of younger trees circled the entire bay, hundreds of meters above the water.
